_comment: Phase 6 M-6.3 piece A — 50-query eval set. Schema in src/lib.rs::EvalCatalog. Corpus is a fictional product-design knowledge base ('Lighthouse', a fake project-tracking product) chosen to be domain-recognizable without referencing real OgreNotes code. The 50 queries split 10 keyword / 15 semantic / 15 multi-hop / 10 gap-analysis per RAG plan §4.1.

corpus.title: Lighthouse Authentication Design
corpus.content:
# Authentication Design

Lighthouse uses OAuth 2.0 with PKCE for browser logins plus a short-lived JWT access token paired with an HttpOnly refresh-token cookie. The access token TTL is 15 minutes; refresh tokens rotate on every use and carry a 30-day idle window. MFA via TOTP is required for admin accounts; optional for everyone else.

corpus.title: Session Management
corpus.content:
# Session Management

Refresh-token rotation happens on every /auth/refresh call. The server writes a new RefreshToken row tied to the previous one's family id; a stolen-then-replayed token mismatches the family and revokes the whole chain. Session expiry is silent — the SPA detects a 401 and bounces to login.

corpus.title: OAuth Provider Integration
corpus.content:
# OAuth Provider Integration

Lighthouse ships with Google and GitHub providers in v1. Each provider gets a separate client-id / client-secret pair stored in SSM Parameter Store; the OAuth callback handler resolves the provider by URL prefix. Token exchange is performed server-side with the access token never crossing the browser boundary.

corpus.title: MFA: TOTP Implementation
corpus.content:
# MFA: TOTP Implementation

Users enable TOTP via /auth/mfa/enroll, which returns a QR-code provisioning URI plus 10 recovery codes. The TOTP secret is stored encrypted at rest. Subsequent logins require both password and a 6-digit code; recovery codes are single-use and rate-limited at 5/minute.

corpus.title: User Data Model
corpus.content:
# User Data Model

Users carry a stable user_id (UUID v4), email (unique within a workspace), display name, role enum (User / Admin), MFA enrolment state, and a home_folder_id. UI prefs (theme, locale, dyslexic_font flag) live in a separate UI_PREFS row. SCIM-provisioned users carry an external_id for the IdP round-trip.

corpus.title: Permission Model
corpus.content:
# Permission Model

Four access levels: Own, Edit, Comment, View. Own is non-transferable in bulk (separate ownership-transfer endpoint required). Permissions are stored as Member rows pointing at folders; document-level access is computed from the folder chain plus per-doc share rows. Link-sharing produces a special 'public link' member row with a configurable expiry.

corpus.title: Document Sharing Specification
corpus.content:
# Document Sharing Specification

Documents can be shared by adding a Member row to the folder or directly to the doc. External link-sharing produces a short, signed URL that grants the configured access level without an account. Bulk-share supports up to 100 docs per call; rejects self-shares and Own-grants.

corpus.title: Folder & Hierarchy Model
corpus.content:
# Folder & Hierarchy Model

Every user gets a Home folder + a Trash folder at signup. Folders form a tree; a doc lives in exactly one folder. Breadcrumbs are computed by walking parent_id pointers. Bulk-move is supported up to 100 docs per call, atomic per id.

corpus.title: Import / Export Formats
corpus.content:
# Import / Export Formats

v1 supports synchronous import for Markdown, HTML, XLSX, and CSV; export for all four plus Markdown round-trips. HTML import runs through ammonia + html5ever sanitization. DOCX and PDF are deferred to v2 because both need the async-worker subsystem to handle the larger conversion latency budget.

corpus.title: Embed URL Allowlist
corpus.content:
# Embed URL Allowlist

Four providers in v1: YouTube, Vimeo, Loom, CodeSandbox. The allowlist matcher converts the user-visible URL to the canonical iframe URL (e.g. youtube.com/watch?v=X → youtube.com/embed/X). Per-workspace allowlist additions are a v2 carry-forward — today's allowlist is global.

corpus.title: Spreadsheet Formula Engine
corpus.content:
# Spreadsheet Formula Engine

The engine ships with 400+ functions across nine categories: arithmetic, statistical, text, logical, date/time, lookup, financial, engineering, and information. Formula evaluation is iterative with a topological sort over the cell dependency graph; circular references resolve to the standard #REF! error.

corpus.title: Pivot Table Implementation
corpus.content:
# Pivot Table Implementation

Pivots support arbitrary row + column dimension nesting plus multi-aggregation values. The compute path operates on a snapshot of the source range; rebuilding the pivot is a single deterministic pass. Subtotals and grand totals render inline with the dimension hierarchy.

corpus.title: Comments & Threads
corpus.content:
# Comments & Threads

Comments anchor to block_ids (and cell ids in spreadsheets). Each thread is a list of messages with timestamps + author. Threads can resolve and reopen. Real-time fanout uses the same WebSocket channel the editor's CRDT updates flow through.

corpus.title: Real-time Collaboration
corpus.content:
# Real-time Collaboration

Documents use the yrs Rust port of Yjs as the CRDT backbone. Updates flow via WebSocket with a binary update protocol. Conflict resolution is automatic per CRDT semantics (last-writer-wins on attributes, structural merge on text). Snapshots persist every N updates to bound recovery time.

corpus.title: User Presence
corpus.content:
# User Presence

Each connected user broadcasts a presence packet (cursor position, selection range, color) on every CRDT awareness update. The color palette is a fixed 16-entry list assigned deterministically by hash(user_id). Stale presence (no packet for 30 s) is gc'd from the awareness map.

corpus.title: Notifications System
corpus.content:
# Notifications System

Activity events (Edit, Comment, Share, Open, Restore, ResolveComment, Delete, Move) emit Notification rows to subscribed users. Three delivery channels: in-app feed (always), browser push (per-user pref), email digest (daily roll-up). Bulk operations should emit aggregate notifications rather than per-id rows — currently a known gap.

corpus.title: Search Architecture
corpus.content:
# Search Architecture

Full-text search uses Tantivy with a two-field schema (title 2x boost, body 1x). Indexing is fire-and-forget on every document mutation. The /search endpoint is permission-filtered with over-fetch — request 20 results, hide rows the user can't view, return up to the originally requested count.

corpus.title: RAG Architecture
corpus.content:
# RAG Architecture

Four-layer system: Tantivy BM25 for keyword, Qdrant vectors for semantic, DynamoDB adjacency list for the knowledge graph, and a Claude tool-calling loop for the agent. Hybrid retrieval uses Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) with default k=60. The agent picks tools based on a system prompt describing each tool's strengths.

corpus.title: Mobile UX Design
corpus.content:
# Mobile UX Design

v1 is responsive web + PWA install + offline indicator. Native iOS / Android are deferred to a future phase. Mobile-specific affordances: 44 px tap targets, drawer-style sidebar on narrow viewports, on-screen formula keyboard for spreadsheet cell editing, and a PWA app shell cached by the service worker (no API caching).

corpus.title: Performance Budgets
corpus.content:
# Performance Budgets

Per-route p95 SLAs codified in CloudWatch alarms. Auth: < 200 ms. Document list: < 300 ms. Search: < 500 ms. WebSocket handshake: < 400 ms. Front-end RUM samples 10% of sessions and POSTs vitals once at 1.5 s post-load. WASM bundle CI gate is set at 2 MiB gzipped (was aspirationally 850 KB; lifted to the as-shipped baseline).

corpus.title: Accessibility Standards
corpus.content:
# Accessibility Standards

The product targets WCAG 2.1 Level AA. Focus traps + role="dialog" + aria-modal on every modal. Live regions (status / alert) for transient state messages. Color contrast verified per design token pair; the contrast audit runs in CI via axe-core. prefers-reduced-motion respected globally via a single CSS media query.

corpus.title: Internationalization Architecture
corpus.content:
# Internationalization Architecture

Fluent (fluent-rs) is the translation harness. Catalogs live in frontend/locales/<locale>/main.ftl. Locale resolution chain: URL ?locale= → user's stored pref → navigator.language → en-US. RTL languages flip layout via logical CSS properties; Arabic ships as the v1 RTL pilot.

corpus.title: Command Palette Design
corpus.content:
# Command Palette Design

Ctrl+K opens the palette in Search mode; Ctrl+Shift+P opens it in Action mode. The action registry is a flat thread_local list. Ranking combines fuzzy match (SkimMatcherV2), scope bonus (+50 for matching the host page's scope), and MRU bonus (+100 decaying −5 per slot, persisted to localStorage).

corpus.title: Bulk Operations Design
corpus.content:
# Bulk Operations Design

Five endpoints: bulk delete / restore / move / share / export. Cap at 100 ids per call. Per-id authorization, no shortcuts. HTTP 207 Multi-Status on partial success; HTTP 200 on all-success. Same JSON body shape both ways so the client doesn't branch on status code to parse.

corpus.title: Knowledge Graph Schema
corpus.content:
# Knowledge Graph Schema

Document relationships live in DynamoDB as adjacency-list rows: REL#<type>#<target_id> for the forward direction, RREL#<type>#<source_id> for the reverse. Five relation types: implements, derived-from, depends-on, references, supersedes. The agent's get_related tool queries this table; today's UI exposes outbound rels only.
